_note: Satellite provider egress routing data. From research: 'GEO to LEO: First Look Into Starlink In-Flight Connectivity' (IMC 2025) and Anthropic geo-restriction docs.

gogo.name: Gogo / Intelsat
gogo.notes: ATG (air-to-ground) in North America, Ku-band GEO satellite globally. Traffic consistently egresses via US ground stations.

inmarsat.name: Inmarsat / Viasat (GX Aviation)
inmarsat.notes: GX gateways in Staines (UK) and Greenwich (US). HEO satellites (GX10) for Arctic coverage launching mid-2026.

viasat.name: Viasat
viasat.notes: Bent-pipe architecture with regional egress. PoPs at DE-CIX Frankfurt, LINX London, US IXPs.

panasonic.name: Panasonic Avionics
panasonic.notes: Integrator using ESA multi-orbit terminals. Primary PoP in Lake Forest, US. Some Asian routes may egress regionally.

starlink.name: SpaceX Starlink Aviation
starlink.notes: ISL (inter-satellite laser) routing can egress on a different continent. Transpacific flights observed egressing in Frankfurt. Egress may change mid-flight. London/Frankfurt have best peering; Milan/Sofia rely on transit.

ses.name: SES O3b mPOWER
ses.notes: Managed gateways with Azure DC adjacencies. ~150ms satellite latency. Regional landing per gateway.

none.name: No WiFi
none.notes: Airline does not offer WiFi (e.g., Ryanair, easyJet, Wizz Air, budget Asian carriers).

unknown.name: Unknown Provider
unknown.notes: WiFi provider not identified. Run /flight-check to test API availability.
